summary: Do not ship. The r8 deadline guard certifies an unsafe 12-app state, its timeout test cannot exercise the real timeout, and its failure rollback can hide a broken producer. No materially simpler complete design was found; the waived r7 finding was not re-raised.

findings.title: APPS cap still permits Jobs that cannot reach the push
findings.body: The formula allows equality: `12×10 + 30 + 30 = 180s`. The pinned Kubernetes controller starts the Job deadline before Pod creation and expires it at `duration >= activeDeadlineSeconds`, so scheduling and startup consume the same 180s. The plan itself budgets startup at 60s, making the admitted worst case 210s. CI can therefore approve a Job that dies before publishing heartbeat/counts, recreating the r8 misattribution. See the [Kubernetes v1.36.2 controller](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/v1.36.2/pkg/controller/job/job_controller.go).
findings.recommendation: Use one strict in-deadline invariant: `pod_start + N×skopeo + curl + execution_slack < activeDeadlineSeconds`, and derive both gates from it. With the stated budgets and 180s deadline, the safe cap is at most 8 unless the deadline is increased.

findings.title: Open question: which seam tests real Skopeo timeout enforcement?
findings.body: A sleeping PATH stub replaces the Skopeo binary that implements `--command-timeout`, so the proposed test cannot make the real timeout fire; emulating it only tests the stub. Moreover, the current command begins `skopeo inspect`, while the pinned CLI requires this global option before `inspect`. The presence-only gate can therefore pass invalid placement, leaving the timeout bound unenforced. See the [Skopeo v1.22.2 CLI contract](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/v1.22.2/docs/skopeo.1.md).
findings.recommendation: Make the stub assert exact argv ordering and simulate a failure for loop semantics; separately smoke-test the pinned Skopeo image against a controlled blackhole to prove bounded completion.

findings.title: Rule-only rollback can hide a broken producer
findings.body: On a late heartbeat or alert, the plan removes only the rules while retaining `Replace`, the Job deadline, and command timeouts. If those producer changes caused missed or partial publication, removing `DigestExporterStale` and `DigestExporterScrapeIncomplete` silences the new detection while `ghcr_latest_digest` remains stale, again blinding `ImageDigestDrift`. This contradicts the full-revert safety contract at line 206.
findings.recommendation: Define cause-specific rollback: rule-only only after proving producer metrics and `ghcr_latest_digest` are healthy; otherwise revert producer and rules together and verify digest publication resumes.

next_steps: Correct the deadline invariant and cardinality cap.
Replace the impossible timeout seam with argument-order and real-binary checks.
Make rollback cause-specific, then rerun the plan review.
